title: When Assignment Matters: A Grounded Study of Compositional Text-to-Image Reranking
description: This project is an incremental but rigorous post-generation study of compositional text-to-image reranking under a fixed SDXL best-of-4 budget. The central claim is narrow and falsifiable: DAAM-assisted slot assignment helps reranking only if it measurably improves explicit slot-assignment accuracy over detector-only and crop-only structured baselines.
motivation: Recent work already covers logic-based reranking, crop-structured inference, and atomic evaluator design, so a strong novelty claim around another generic compositional verifier would not be credible. The remaining gap is to isolate whether reranking errors are primarily assignment errors, and whether generator-native attention traces provide useful assignment evidence beyond detector confidence and crop-only scoring.
proposed_approach: Generate a shared cache of `200 prompts x 3 seeds x 4 candidates = 2400` SDXL images, parse each prompt into object groups plus count, attribute, and relation atoms, and detect candidate boxes with GroundingDINO-Tiny. Capture lightweight SDXL DAAM traces online by averaging only the final 10 denoising steps for parsed phrase spans, cache them as compressed phrase heatmaps, and combine DAAM box mass with detector confidence in a capacitated assignment solver with explicit null nodes and exchangeable repeated-object slots; then verify assigned regions with in-prompt SigLIP counterfactuals for attributes and geometric tests for relations before reranking with a shared soft conjunction.

related_work.title: LogicRank: Logic Induced Reranking for Generative Text-to-Image Systems
related_work.authors: Bjorn Deiseroth, Patrick Schramowski, Hikaru Shindo, Devendra Singh Dhami, Kristian Kersting
related_work.how_it_relates: Closest reranking precursor; it already performs logic-aware reranking, so this project only claims novelty if explicit assignment accuracy becomes the main measured variable and DAAM materially improves that variable.

related_work.title: Adding simple structure at inference improves Vision-Language Compositionality
related_work.authors: Imanol Miranda, Ander Salaberria, Eneko Agirre, Gorka Azkune
related_work.how_it_relates: Strong crop-structured baseline that already shows structured inference helps; this proposal must show that DAAM-assisted assignment adds something beyond crop-only composition.

related_work.title: T2I-FineEval: Fine-Grained Compositional Metric for Text-to-Image Evaluation
related_work.authors: Seyed Mohammad Hadi Hosseini, Amir Mohammad Izadi, Ali Abdollahi, Armin Saghafian, Mahdieh Soleymani Baghshah
related_work.how_it_relates: Recent evaluator that decomposes text and image into fine-grained question-component pairs; it narrows the novelty space for any post-hoc verifier claim.

related_work.title: CompAlign: Improving Compositional Text-to-Image Generation with a Complex Benchmark and Fine-Grained Feedback
related_work.how_it_relates: Introduces the CompAlign benchmark and CompQuest atomic feedback evaluator, showing that fine-grained post-hoc compositional evaluation is already active research.

related_work.title: What the DAAM: Interpreting Stable Diffusion Using Cross Attention
related_work.authors: Raphael Tang, Linqing Liu, Akshat Pandey, Zhiying Jiang, Gefei Yang, Karun Kumar, Pontus Stenetorp, Jimmy Lin, Ferhan Ture
related_work.how_it_relates: Provides the generator-native attention signal used here as a noisy auxiliary cue; the paper explicitly tests whether this interpretability signal is stable enough to help assignment.

related_work.title: Token Merging for Training-Free Semantic Binding in Text-to-Image Synthesis
related_work.authors: Taihang Hu, Linxuan Li, Joost van de Weijer, Hongcheng Gao, Fahad Khan, Jian Yang, Ming-Ming Cheng, Kai Wang, Yaxing Wang
related_work.how_it_relates: A generation-time training-free binding method that occupies the semantic-binding novelty space this proposal is not claiming.

related_work.title: VSC: Visual Search Compositional Text-to-Image Diffusion Model
related_work.authors: Do Huu Dat, Hyeon-Woo Nam, Po-Yuan Mao, Tae-Hyun Oh
related_work.how_it_relates: A compositional generation model that changes the generation process rather than reranking a fixed candidate pool.

related_work.title: R-Bind: Unified Enhancement of Attribute and Relation Binding in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
related_work.how_it_relates: A training-free generation-time binding method that further pushes this proposal toward a narrower post-generation analysis framing.

related_work.title: GenEval: An Object-Focused Framework for Evaluating Text-to-Image Alignment
related_work.authors: Dhruba Ghosh, Hannaneh Hajishirzi, Ludwig Schmidt
related_work.how_it_relates: Primary benchmark source for count, color, and position evaluation.

related_work.title: T2I-CompBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Open-world Compositional Text-to-image Generation
related_work.authors: Kaiyi Huang, Kaiyue Sun, Enze Xie, Zhenguo Li, Xihui Liu
related_work.how_it_relates: Primary benchmark source for attribute-binding and spatial-relation prompts compatible with explicit 2D assignment.

hypothesis: On a shared `2400`-image SDXL cache, DAAM-assisted assignment plus in-prompt counterfactual verification will improve final compositional reranking only when it first improves explicit slot-assignment accuracy over detector-only structured reranking and crop-only structured scoring.
success_criteria: The full method improves slot-assignment accuracy by at least 8 absolute points over detector-only structured assignment on a manually annotated 100 prompt-image pair set.
The full method improves all-atoms-pass by at least 3 absolute points over the strongest non-DAAM structured baseline on the shared prompt-seed evaluation set.
The full method improves GenEval overall accuracy by at least 4 absolute points over best-of-4 global SigLIP reranking on the same cached candidates.
Mechanism-supporting analyses show measurable gains in group-count accuracy or null-decision accuracy, rather than gains arising only from holistic tie-breaking.

The complete experiment set, including the dev run and a 40-prompt best-of-8 subset, remains feasible within the single-A6000 approximately 8-hour budget because DAAM traces, detections, and crop features are cached and reused.
